svg.title: Failure modes of consensus
svg.desc: Four named failure modes. Lossy synthesis: the judge drops a real finding a member caught, so the merge under-performs its best member. Shared hallucination: frontier models share training data and fail together on a common blind spot. False consensus: agreement measures correlation, not truth, so unanimous ungrounded answers can be confidently wrong. Precision cost: more models means more flags, some nitpicks, that must be triaged. On routine tasks consensus can be worse than a single model.
svg.text: On routine, saturated tasks a single strong model can be the better choice.
